ocr.text: In the BASF SE Chemicals & Materials 3rd Quarter 2023 report, the Carbon footprint metrics reveal Scope1Emissions at
500,000 tCO2e, Scope2Emissions at 200,000 tCO2e, Scope3Emissions at 1,500,000 tCO2e, and TotalEmissions at 2,200,000
tCO2e, with a ReductionTarget of 25% and current Progress at 85%. Achieved metrics show Scope1Emissions at 300,000
tCO2e, Scope2Emissions at 150,000 tCO2e, Scope3Emissions at 1,200,000 tCO2e, leading to TotalEmissions of 1,650,000
tCO2e, translating to a 30% reduction.
Metric  Value  Change  Target  CarbonFootprintMetrics  Unit
Scope1Emissions  Scope2Emissions  Scope3Emissions  TotalEmissions  ReductionTarget  Progress  Status
The category marked OnTrack indicates Scope1Emissions at 700,000 tCO2e, Scope2Emissions at 350,000 tCO2e, and
Scope3Emissions at 3,000,000 tCO2e, culminating in TotalEmissions of 4,050,000 tCO2e, with a 20% reduction and 75%
Progress, categorized as BehindTarget, with Scope1Emissions recorded at 1,000,000 tCO2e and TotalEmissions reaching
4,500,000 tCO2e while maintaining a 6,000,000 tCO2e threshold. The AtRisk category shows Scope1Emissions at 1,200,000
tCO2e and total 600,000 tCO2e emissions, reflecting a 15% reduction and 70% Progress. Meanwhile, the Petrochemical
Cracker Utilization table reports Product amounts of Ethylene at 2000 EURM with a YoYChange of 5.0%, Propylene at 1500
EURM with a 4.5% change, and Butadiene at 800 EURM with a 3.0% change, leading to TotalProduction of 5300 EURM and a
UtilizationRate of 85%.

TotalCapacity stands at 7400 EURM, with OperationalEfficiency at 92%. MarketTrends are Stable, FutureProjections indicate
an Increase, with an Investment of 250 EURM in NewProjects, while Feedback remains Positive amidst SupplyChain
Challenges and Regulations.
